name: Sestri Levante
body:
Sestri Levante's geography does the structural work — a narrow isthmus with two bays, the Baia del Silenzio facing east into the Andersen-fable register and the Baia delle Favole facing west toward the wider sand strip. Five nights at Hotel Helvetia put both bays inside a five-minute walk; the old town's pesto trattorie and pasticcerie sit on the same isthmus, which means the daily-life loop runs entirely on foot. The transit in from Alba routes through Recco for the IGP-protected focaccia at Manuelina, a 15-min detour off the A12 that costs nothing in beach time.

The active half-day is Punta Manara — three miles round trip, ~700 ft elevation, the Saracen watchtower at the top. The trip's strongest single Liguria-cultural pick lands on Monday Jun 1: the Camogli ferry to the Abbazia di San Fruttuoso, a 30-minute boat into a cove only reachable by sea or footpath. Lunch at the abbey, the Cristo degli Abissi viewing if the freedive map is at the dock, frittura marina from a paper cone on the Camogli quay on the way back. The lazy Sunday lunch runs inland to Lavagna at Agriturismo Olivenere — coniglio alla ligure, torta pasqualina, the estate's olive oil.

anchors.name: Baia del Silenzio
anchors.blurb: The smaller, quieter bay on the isthmus's east face — the Andersen register

anchors.name: Sentiero per Punta Manara
anchors.blurb: 3 miles round-trip, Saracen watchtower; trailhead at Vico del Bottone

anchors.name: Abbazia di San Fruttuoso
anchors.blurb: 13th-century abbey reachable only by sea or footpath; FAI-run, 30-min ferry from Camogli

anchors.name: Basilica di Santa Maria Assunta, Camogli
anchors.blurb: Pastel-fishing-village register; the anti-Portofino harbor walk
